name: St. John's

confRecord.note: Missed Big East Tournament. Georgetown won Big East Tourn back-to-back.

notable: NYC pro network — NYCFC, Red Bulls academy access
proPlayers.draftRank: Consistent D1 producer; NYC clinical network unique advantage

acuAlignNote: Core exercise science covered. NYC location provides extraordinary clinical placement access for DPT applications — Hospital for Special Surgery, NYU Langone, NY Presbyterian all accessible.

gpa.note: St. John's is accessible for international student-athletes. GPA 2.8 is fine.

fin.internationalNote: St. John's offers multiple merit scholarship programs. Athletic + Catholic Mission Scholarship can stack well for international students.

culture.vibe: New York City campus culture — fast-paced, ambitious, diverse, and never dull. Queens is genuine NYC — the most ethnically diverse urban county in the world. Campus itself is a green oasis but step outside and you're in one of the world's great cities.
culture.thingsToDo: Central Park, Manhattan skyline, Brooklyn Bridge, NYCFC/Red Bulls soccer, MSG events, Broadway, Museum Mile, Queens food scene (most diverse cuisine on earth), Citi Field, every type of music venue imaginable. NYC needs no introduction.
culture.socialScene: Extremely diverse student body. Every culture represented. Catholic university ethos but applied liberally. Commuter culture means less tight-knit than residential campuses. City is the campus in many ways.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ NYC is incomparable for urban experience. Weather is cold (winters) which is the main lifestyle mismatch. If Olivier can handle cold winters, the city culture and clinical network are extraordinary.

facilityDetails.stadium: Belson Stadium — 2,168 capacity, dedicated soccer facility in Queens NYC. FieldTurf, private suites, press box, lights, permanent seating. Hosts NPSL (Osners FC) from 2025.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields at Belson complex. St. John's Athletics also uses Lou Carnesecca Arena outdoor areas for overflow training. Limited by NYC land constraints but well-maintained.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: St. John's Carnesecca Arena Athletic Weight Room — 12,000 sq ft, full free weight section, cable systems, functional training area. Dedicated performance coach for soccer. Recovery suite with normatec boots and contrast bath access through athletic training room.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports) for all sessions. Video analysis via Hudl. Department of Human Performance and Exercise Science has biomechanics teaching lab and exercise physiology lab.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Dedicated certified athletic trainer. Hospital for Special Surgery (HSS — ranked #1 orthopaedic hospital in the world, 14 consecutive years) has a partnership with St. John's Athletics. NYU Langone Sports Performance Center and NY Presbyterian Hospital are both accessible. Best sports medicine access of any school in this guide from a graduate school observation perspective.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: St. John's School of Education Human Performance: exercise physiology lab, anatomy lab, biomechanics teaching space. NYC clinical placement network is extraordinary — students can access HSS, NYU Langone, and NY Presbyterian for DPT observation hours without leaving the city.
facilityDetails.extras: Barclays Center and Madison Square Garden — two of the world's great arenas within 30 minutes. NYCFC and NY Red Bulls training ground access for players with agent connections. NYC's unmatched cultural offering: every museum, park, restaurant, and music venue. Metropolitan Museum of Art, Central Park, Brooklyn Bridge all accessible by subway.
facilityDetails.note: Belson Stadium and the athletic facilities are functional but not elite by D1 standards. The extraordinary differentiator is the NYC clinical network — Hospital for Special Surgery is the #1 orthopaedic hospital in the world and St. John's players can access it for clinical observation.

coach.name: Dr Dave Masur
coach.title: Head Coach / Associate Athletics Director (35th season at SJU)
coach.profile: 35th season at St Johns 2025. 39th season as HC overall. 468-213-118 (.660) career record. 415-192-112 at SJU. 1996 NCAA National Champion. 4 College Cups. 23 NCAA Tournaments including 15 consecutive 1992-2006. 9 Big East Tournament titles. 2x National CoY (1996, 2001). 3x Big East CoY. Doctor of Education from St Johns. 2025: returned to NCAA Tournament for first time since 2021, 9-6-4 record. 2nd-winningest active D1 HC.

rec: NYC = Hospital for Special Surgery, NYU Langone, NY Presbyterian — best clinical DPT access in the country. Big East soccer is tough. Cool climate. City lifestyle partially compensates.

staff.name: David Janezic
staff.bg: Associate HC since 2021. 9th year on staff 2025. Named TopDrawerSoccer Top D1 Assistant multiple times. Part of 2019 Big East Coaching Staff of Year. Developed Nicolas Fleuriau Chateau (3rd round MLS pick, Vancouver, 2024). Previously at Fairleigh Dickinson.

name: Creighton
acuAlignNote: Solid exercise science — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics covered. CHI Health and Creighton Medical have strong pre-PT placement.

fin.internationalNote: Creighton full COA is ~$69k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–50% of COA. Catholic mission scholarship may stack for qualifying students. Omaha has low cost of living — package goes further than the sticker suggests. Big East platform with national visibility.

gpa.note: Jesuit university with strong academic expectations. Athletic scholarship well-established.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for Big East Tournament.

coach.name: Johnny Torres
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Multiple Big East titles. One of the premier programs in the conference. 2022 Big East Tournament Champions under Schmitt. Creighton consistently competes above its market in national recruiting.

facilityDetails.stadium: Morrison Stadium — 7,500 capacity (shared soccer/football), FieldTurf, lights, permanent seating. Substantial facility for a small university.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Dedicated practice fields at Morrison. Indoor training facility access in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Creighton Athletics S&C — standard D1 suite. Olympic lifting, full weight room. Dedicated performance coach for soccer.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs — biomechanics, exercise physiology, sport performance. Creighton Health Sciences campus provides strong research integration.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: CHI Health (Creighton Medicine) — team physicians and athletic trainers. One of the better pre-PT clinical environments in the Big East outside NYC.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs: physiology lab, biomechanics lab. Creighton Health Sciences campus integration for pre-PT pathway.
facilityDetails.extras: Omaha is a genuine, affordable Midwest city — College World Series host, TD Ameritrade ballpark, Old Market district. 8 hours to Chicago. Creighton Jesuit ethos creates strong alumni community.
facilityDetails.note: Morrison Stadium is the best dedicated soccer facility in this Big East batch. Omaha's low cost of living maximises aid package value. CHI Health provides a solid pre-PT environment.

culture.vibe: Jesuit university with a tight-knit community. Omaha is a genuinely liveable Midwest city — low cost, friendly, safe. College World Series culture every June. Creighton athletics pride is high.
culture.thingsToDo: Old Market historic district (restaurants, galleries), College World Series (June), Henry Doorly Zoo (top 5 world), Omaha Symphony, TD Ameritrade Park. Kansas City day trip (5hr), Chicago (8hr).
culture.socialScene: Small campus (9,000) creates tight community. Jesuit ethos is present but applied practically. International student community exists but the culture is primarily domestic American.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Omaha is affordable and friendly but far from Sydney lifestyle. Low cost of living maximises aid. Cold winters, no beach, flat landscape. Best suited if value and soccer quality are the main drivers.
culture.lifestyleTags: Affordable Omaha, Jesuit community, value play

staff.name: Ted Schmitt
staff.bg: Head Coach. Multiple Big East titles. Premier Big East program builder.

rec: Consistently strong Big East program. Omaha is a real city with low cost of living. Good value for a competitive D1 program.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 of 12 MFs clear before Olivier arrives. 7 remain — 4 as seniors in Yr1, 3 as juniors for 2 years. Head coach is Johnny Torres (appointed 2025). Creighton has a solid Big East history with active JUCO transfer pipeline.

name: Providence
acuAlignNote: Health policy and management pathway. Pre-PT advising available but less structured than kinesiology-specific programs.

fin.internationalNote: Providence full COA is ~$72k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Catholic mission scholarship may stack. Providence, RI has moderate cost of living. Merit aid available for strong academic records.

gpa.note: Small Catholic liberal arts college. Athletic scholarship pathway available.

confRecord.note: 4th seed Big East Tourn. Lost SF to Georgetown 0-2.

coach.name: Craig Stewart
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Developing Providence within competitive Big East field. 2025 4th seed in Big East Tournament — best result in recent years. Providence, RI location gives Boston day-trip access and solid New England soccer culture.

facilityDetails.stadium: Glay Field — 1,500 capacity, natural grass. Modest dedicated facility on PC campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields on campus. Indoor access in winter at Mullaney Gymnasium.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: PC Athletics weight room — standard small-school D1 suite. Full weight training, recovery facilities.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Health Sciences department labs. Basic exercise physiology and anatomy resources. Providence proximity to Brown University and Rhode Island Hospital creates research adjacency.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: PC Athletics trainers. Providence VA Medical Center and Lifespan hospital network for clinical access.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Health Policy and Management: health sciences labs, research methods labs. Pre-PT pathway requires external clinical placements — Providence city and Boston access helpful.
facilityDetails.extras: Providence is a genuine New England city — RISD (Rhode Island School of Design) creates vibrant arts culture. Boston 50 min by car/train. Thayer Street and Federal Hill restaurant scene.
facilityDetails.note: Small Catholic college facilities without elite extras. The location (Providence, Boston access) and Big East soccer platform are the key assets.

culture.vibe: Small tight-knit Catholic college town feel despite being in a real city. Providence is genuinely charming — East Coast character, walkable, safe, with a massive arts and food scene. PC community is close and loyal.
culture.thingsToDo: Providence waterfront, WaterFire art installation, Federal Hill (excellent Italian restaurants), RISD Museum, Boston day trip (50 min), Cape Cod (2hr), Newport (30 min), Roger Williams Zoo.
culture.socialScene: Tight-knit small campus (4,000). Strong athletic culture. Boston proximity expands social options significantly. Catholic ethos is present without being restrictive.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Providence is a nice city but the academic program is weaker for Olivier's PT pathway. Expensive for the program tier. Best if New England culture resonates and Boston access is valued.
culture.lifestyleTags: Providence charm, Boston access, small campus

staff.name: Craig Stewart
staff.bg: Head Coach. Developing Providence in competitive Big East.

rec: Small Catholic college. Providence is a real city with Boston day-trip access. Expensive for the program tier.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 strong rising juniors dominate MF competition in Yr1–Yr2, departing after 2028-29. Providence had a strong 2025 (4th Big East, beat Georgetown semi). Coach Craig Stewart confirmed.

name: Seton Hall
acuAlignNote: Exercise science program covers core anatomy and physiology. NYC proximity (30 min) opens extensive clinical placement options for pre-PT.

fin.internationalNote: Seton Hall full COA is ~$68k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Catholic mission scholarship may stack. NJ proximity to NYC raises living costs. Merit aid available. Strong health science and pre-professional programs.

gpa.note: Accessible Catholic university. Athletic support well-established.

confRecord.note: Did not qualify for Big East Tournament.

coach.name: Andreas Lindberg
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Building Seton Hall in competitive Big East field. NYC metro (30 min to Manhattan) is a genuine program differentiator — NY Red Bulls and NYCFC access for players. 2021 Big East Tournament Champions.

facilityDetails.stadium: Owen T. Carroll Field — 1,500 capacity, artificial turf. Dedicated soccer facility on SHU campus in South Orange.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields at Carroll Field. Walsh Gymnasium indoor access in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Seton Hall Athletics weight room — standard Catholic D1 suite. Full weight training, recovery facilities.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics labs. Basic but functional teaching labs. NYC access enhances clinical quality.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Hackensack Meridian Health — team physicians and athletic trainers. NYC hospital network for clinical placements: Hospital for Special Surgery, NYU Langone accessible from campus.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs on campus. Pre-PT pathway relies partly on NYC clinical placements — commuting 30 min to Manhattan opens HSS, NYU Langone access.
facilityDetails.extras: New York City 30 min by NJ Transit — Manhattan, Red Bulls Arena, Barclays Center all accessible. South Orange is a pleasant NJ suburb. Seton Hall basketball (Big East powerhouse) creates excellent athletics culture.
facilityDetails.note: SHU's facilities are modest but NYC proximity elevates clinical value enormously. Red Bulls and NYCFC access for players. Hackensack Meridian Health for sports medicine.

culture.vibe: Catholic university in a pleasant NJ suburb with New York City 30 minutes away. Campus is quiet and leafy but NYC is the real backyard. Big East basketball culture (Seton Hall is a basketball school) elevates athletics identity.
culture.thingsToDo: New York City (30 min NJ Transit) — everything. NY Red Bulls (Harrison, 40 min), NYCFC, MoMA, Central Park, Brooklyn food scene, Newark Performing Arts Center (20 min). South Orange itself is charming.
culture.socialScene: Mid-size Catholic campus. Strong sports culture especially basketball. International students present. NYC proximity means social life extends far beyond campus.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ NYC access is genuine and valuable for Olivier's PT pathway. Program tier and cost-effectiveness are the main trade-offs. Best if NYC lifestyle and clinical access are priorities over program prestige.
culture.lifestyleTags: NYC adjacent, Catholic campus, Big East basketball culture

staff.name: Ryan Baker
staff.bg: Head Coach. Building SHU in Big East. 2021 Big East Tournament Champions.

rec: NYC proximity is the main draw — 30 min to Manhattan. Mid-tier Big East program. Expensive for the tier.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 6 of 8 MFs clear before Olivier arrives — only Sansone and Mana remain as Yr1 seniors. Spring 2026 squad is very small; recruiting class will shape competition. Program in transition under new head coach Andreas Lindberg.

name: Butler
acuAlignNote: Strong exercise science program. Indianapolis medical district nearby — IU Health and Ascension provide pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: Butler full COA is ~$72k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–50% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with strong academic record. Indianapolis cost of living is moderate — stretches the package further. Small campus with strong athletics culture.

gpa.note: Selective small private university. Athletic scholarships available for internationals.

confRecord.note: Last in Big East Midwest Division.

coach.name: Paul Snape
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Small university punching above weight in the Big East. Butler's beautiful campus and Indianapolis medical district create a strong environment for pre-health athletes. IU Health and Ascension are leading clinical placement sites.

facilityDetails.stadium: Butler Bowl — 5,000 capacity, natural grass. Beautiful setting on Butler's stunning campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields on campus. Hinkle Fieldhouse indoor access in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Sellick Bowl area — Butler Athletics S&C facility. Olympic lifting, full weight training. Dedicated coach for soccer.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs. Biomechanics, physiology labs available. Indianapolis medical district integration for research access.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: IU Health and Ascension hospitals — affiliated network for team physician and clinical access. One of the better pre-PT clinical environments in this Big East batch.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science: anatomy lab, physiology lab, biomechanics lab. IU Health clinical placement pathway available through Butler pre-health advising.
facilityDetails.extras: Butler Bowl on one of the most beautiful small university campuses in America. Indiana Pacers, Indianapolis Colts proximity. Indianapolis Motor Speedway (Indy 500). Butler Bulldogs basketball is a Big East powerhouse.
facilityDetails.note: Butler's campus is genuinely beautiful — described as one of the best small university campuses in the USA. IU Health clinical access makes this strong for Olivier's PT pathway. Indianapolis medical district is a genuine asset.

culture.vibe: Intimate, beautiful private university campus in the middle of a real Midwest city. Butler has an outsized athletics culture for its size — Big East basketball powerhouse. Indianapolis is a genuine sports city.
culture.thingsToDo: Indianapolis downtown (Mass Ave arts district), Indiana Pacers NBA, Indianapolis Colts NFL, Indy 500, Lucas Oil Stadium concerts, Indianapolis Motor Speedway, Children's Museum (largest in world), IU Health facilities.
culture.socialScene: Tight-knit small campus (5,000). Strong athletics community. Indianapolis is larger and more liveable than many expect — growing food and arts scene. International students supported.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Beautiful campus + Indianapolis city. Best Big East value for exercise science. IU Health access for PT pathway. Cold Midwest winters but the campus environment is genuinely special.
culture.lifestyleTags: Beautiful campus, Indianapolis city, IU Health access

staff.name: Paul Snape
staff.bg: Head Coach. Building Butler's Big East standing with a beautiful campus advantage.

rec: Beautiful small campus in Indianapolis. Good value for a Big East program. Indianapolis medical district for pre-PT clinical access.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Only 2 MFs clear before arrival, BUT 6 are rising seniors who all depart after Yr1 — creating a major opening in Yr2. Butler is Big East mid-lower tier. IU Health clinical access is the academic draw.

name: Xavier
acuAlignNote: Exercise science covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Xavier's pre-health advising connects to Cincinnati medical district clinical options.

fin.internationalNote: Xavier full COA is ~$58k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Catholic mission scholarship may stack. Cincinnati has affordable cost of living — package goes further. Merit aid available for international students with strong academic records.

gpa.note: Jesuit university with flexible admission. Athletic scholarship well-established.

confRecord.note: 1st in Big East Midwest Division — Big East Tournament run.

coach.name: John Higgins
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Developing Xavier program in competitive Big East. Won Big East Midwest Division in 2023. FC Cincinnati MLS relationship for local pro soccer access.

facilityDetails.stadium: Corcoran Field — 1,000 capacity, natural grass. Dedicated soccer on Xavier campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields on campus. Cintas Center indoor access in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Xavier Athletics weight room — standard small D1 suite.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs. Anatomy, physiology, biomechanics teaching labs.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: University of Cincinnati Health / TriHealth — team physician access. Cincinnati Medical Center pre-PT clinical placements available.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs: physiology, biomechanics. Cincinnati Children's Hospital (one of the top 5 children's hospitals in USA) creates research adjacency for health science students.
facilityDetails.extras: FC Cincinnati MLS (TQL Stadium, 6,000 cap) is 10 min from campus. Cincinnati is a genuine Midwest city with a growing food and arts scene. Cincinnati Zoo, Eden Park, Columbia Tusculum neighbourhood.
facilityDetails.note: Xavier's best differentiator is FC Cincinnati proximity — real MLS access. Cincinnati Medical Center for pre-PT. Small campus, tight community.

culture.vibe: Small Jesuit university with a punching-above-weight athletics culture. Xavier is known for basketball but soccer is building. Cincinnati has become a genuinely liveable Midwest city with a strong culture.
culture.thingsToDo: FC Cincinnati MLS (10 min), Cincinnati Reds MLB, Great American Ball Park, Cincinnati Zoo (top 3 USA), Eden Park, Newport Kentucky riverfront (across Ohio River), Cincinnati Art Museum, OTR (Over The Rhine — one of the best food/bar neighbourhoods in the Midwest).
culture.socialScene: Small campus (7,000). Tight Jesuit community. Cincinnati has a real social scene — OTR neighbourhood especially. FC Cincinnati creates strong soccer culture in the city.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Cincinnati is better than most expect — FC Cincinnati MLS is genuinely cool. Cincinnati Children's Hospital research proximity. Cold winters. Exercise science program adequate but not elite.
culture.lifestyleTags: FC Cincinnati access, Cincinnati city, Jesuit community

staff.name: Nate Winding
staff.bg: Head Coach. Developing Xavier in Big East. FC Cincinnati relationship.

rec: Small Jesuit university in Cincinnati. FC Cincinnati proximity is a pro soccer bonus. Mid-tier Big East program.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 of 9 MFs clear. Xavier is lower-half Big East but won Midwest Division in 2023. FC Cincinnati MLS (10 min from campus) is a program asset. Head coach confirmed: John Higgins.

name: UConn
acuAlignNote: Solid kinesiology — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. UConn Health Center provides strong pre-PT clinical pathway.

fin.internationalNote: UConn out-of-state COA is ~$52k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Connecticut cost of living is moderate. Strong health sciences and kinesiology pathway relevant to Olivier's DPT goals.

gpa.note: Public university accessible for international athletes. Strong D1 athletic tradition.

confRecord.note: 3rd seed Big East Tourn. Reached final, lost to Georgetown 1-3.
Top Big East seed. NCAA Tournament.
Big East regular season title. NCAA Tournament.

coach.name: Chris Gbandi
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Legendary UConn coach with multiple Big East titles and NCAA tournament appearances. Reached 2025 Big East Final before losing to Georgetown 1-3. Consistently top-3 in the Big East. UConn Health integration for pre-PT pathway.

facilityDetails.stadium: Joseph J. Morrone Stadium at Rizza Performance Center — 3,000 capacity, dedicated soccer, on-campus. Rizza Performance Center attached: dedicated S&C, sports medicine, locker rooms and player lounge for soccer programs. New student-athlete success centre (Bailey Center) breaking ground 2025.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated practice fields adjacent to DeLuca. Indoor facility at Olympic Sports Center in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: UConn Olympic Sports Center — full S&C suite. Olympic lifting, full recovery facilities. Dedicated strength coach for soccer.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Kinesiology Department labs — biomechanics, exercise physiology, motor control. UConn Health Center provides research integration.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UConn Health Center — team physicians and athletic trainers. Pre-PT clinical pathway through UConn School of Medicine affiliation.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Kinesiology Building: exercise physiology lab, biomechanics lab, motor control lab. UConn Health Center clinical placements accessible.
facilityDetails.extras: Hartford (40 min) and Boston (2hr) provide city access. UConn's other Big East titles give enormous program credibility. Husky Nation basketball creates exceptional Big East athletics culture.
facilityDetails.note: UConn is the strongest Big East soccer program outside Georgetown and Notre Dame. DeLuca Field is a solid dedicated facility. UConn Health for pre-PT is the academic differentiator. Rural Storrs is the main lifestyle trade-off.

culture.vibe: Large public research university with a strong athletics culture. Husky basketball is legendary — UConn is a true Big East athletics school. Storrs is a quintessential isolated New England college campus.
culture.thingsToDo: Hartford (40 min), Boston (2hr), Providence (90 min), Hartford Yard Goats baseball, UConn basketball (national championships), Connecticut shoreline (Long Island Sound, 90 min), skiing at Mohawk Mountain.
culture.socialScene: Mid-large campus (19,000 undergrad). Strong athletics community. International student support. Storrs itself is isolated — the university is the town.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Isolated rural campus, cold Connecticut winters. Strong soccer program (top-3 Big East). UConn Health for PT pathway is solid. Best if program quality and value are primary drivers over lifestyle.
culture.lifestyleTags: Rural New England, Big East soccer, Husky athletics

staff.name: Ray Reid
staff.bg: Head Coach. Legendary UConn coach. Consistent Big East top-3.

rec: Historically strong program. Rural Storrs campus is the main drawback — isolated location in Connecticut.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: UConn reached the 2025 Big East Final (lost to Georgetown 1–3). 5 MFs remain on arrival plus strong incoming recruits. Yr3 is the realistic starting point. Head coach confirmed: Chris Gbandi.

name: DePaul
acuAlignNote: Health Sciences pathway covers anatomy, physiology, nutrition. Chicago medical district provides outstanding pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: DePaul full COA is ~$69k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Vincentian Catholic mission scholarship available. Merit aid available for strong academic records. Chicago living costs are high — factor into net cost.

gpa.note: Jesuit university with athletic scholarship pathway.

proPlayers.draftRank: Chicago Fire proximity a development pathway

coach.name: Mark Plotkin
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Building DePaul in competitive Big East. Chicago location is the program's strongest asset — Chicago Fire MLS access, Lurie Children's Hospital, Rush University Medical Center proximity. Two last-place Midwest finishes signal a program needing development.

facilityDetails.stadium: Wish Field (Lincoln Park Campus) — dedicated soccer field. DePaul plays in downtown Chicago Lincoln Park neighbourhood.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Training at Wish Field and Chicago area facilities. Indoor access at DePaul's Ray Meyer Fitness Center.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Ray Meyer Fitness Center — standard Catholic D1 suite. Full weight training, recovery access.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Health Sciences Department labs. Anatomy, physiology, nutrition labs. Chicago medical district proximity enables research access beyond campus.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Rush University Medical Center — DePaul's academic medical partner. Lurie Children's Hospital, Northwestern Medicine all accessible from Lincoln Park campus.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Health Sciences labs on campus. Chicago's medical district proximity is the differentiator — Rush, Northwestern Medicine, Lurie Children's all within commuting distance for clinical hours.
facilityDetails.extras: Lincoln Park location — DePaul is IN Chicago. Chicago Fire MLS (Wintrust Arena), restaurants, music venues, Lake Michigan shoreline, Art Institute of Chicago, Second City comedy. Chicago is world class.
facilityDetails.note: DePaul's facilities are modest but Chicago Lincoln Park location is genuinely exceptional. Rush University Medical Center partnership for health sciences. Best Big East lifestyle location after Georgetown.

culture.vibe: DePaul is the only Big East soccer program actually located IN a world-class city — Lincoln Park, Chicago. Campus culture blends Vincentian Catholic ethos with Chicago's diverse, creative energy. City IS the campus.
culture.thingsToDo: Chicago Fire MLS, Chicago Cubs (Wrigley, 20 min walk), Chicago Bulls NBA, Chicago Blackhawks NHL, Art Institute, Lincoln Park Zoo, Lake Shore Drive, Millennium Park, Chicago dining (world-class), live music. Chicago is limitless.
culture.socialScene: Medium campus (14,000) in one of the world's great cities. DePaul attracts diverse students who want Chicago. Soccer team is small but tight-knit. International students well-represented.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Best lifestyle of any Big East program for Olivier — Chicago is genuinely close to Sydney energy in terms of world-class city experience. Rush Medical for PT pathway. Soccer program is weakest in the conference — that is the real trade-off.
culture.lifestyleTags: Chicago city, Lincoln Park, world-class lifestyle

staff.name: Mark Plotkin
staff.bg: Head Coach. Building DePaul in Big East with Chicago as the strongest program asset.

rec: Chicago city life is the main appeal — strongest lifestyle score in this Big East batch. Weakest soccer in the conference but best location for Olivier.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Best minutes opportunity in the Big East — 5 of 8 MFs clear. Only 3 senior MFs remain in Yr1 then depart. DePaul is weakest Big East Midwest program (last place twice). Chicago Lincoln Park location is the main draw. Olivier at A-League U18 level can compete immediately.

name: Villanova
acuAlignNote: Exercise science and human services pathways. Philadelphia medical district (30 min) for pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: Villanova full COA is ~$72k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Catholic mission scholarship may stack. Main Line Philadelphia has moderate-to-high living costs. Merit aid available for strong academic records.

gpa.note: Selective Catholic university. Athletic scholarship pathway available.

coach.name: Mark Fetrow
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Developing Villanova within competitive Big East environment. Three consecutive last-place East Division finishes signal a program in transition. Philadelphia medical district (Jefferson Health, Penn Medicine, CHOP) creates strong pre-PT clinical access.

facilityDetails.stadium: Villanova Soccer Complex — dedicated facility on Main Line campus. 1,500 capacity, natural grass.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice fields at Villanova Soccer Complex. Pavilion indoor facility access in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Villanova Athletics weight room — standard Catholic D1 suite.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs on campus. Philadelphia medical district research adjacency.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Jefferson Health (Villanova Athletics partner) — team physicians. Philadelphia area hospitals (Penn Medicine, Thomas Jefferson, CHOP) accessible for pre-PT clinical hours.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs: physiology, biomechanics. Pre-PT pathway through Philadelphia medical district — CHOP (Children's Hospital of Philadelphia — top 2 USA) and Penn Medicine accessible.
facilityDetails.extras: Main Line Philadelphia — one of the most prestigious suburban locations in the USA. Philadelphia Eagles, 76ers, Phillies, Union (MLS) all nearby. King of Prussia (largest mall east of Mississippi, 10 min). Philadelphia cultural district.
facilityDetails.note: Villanova's academic prestige and Philadelphia medical access are the key assets. Soccer is the weakest element — three consecutive last-place East finishes. Expensive for the program tier.

culture.vibe: Prestigious small Catholic university on Philadelphia's Main Line — one of the most affluent, culturally rich suburban corridors in the USA. Villanova athletics culture is basketball-dominant (Wildcats are Big East royalty) but soccer is building.
culture.thingsToDo: Philadelphia Union MLS (30 min), Philadelphia Eagles/76ers/Phillies, Independence Hall/Liberty Bell, Penn Museum, Fairmount Park, Reading Terminal Market, South Philly food scene, King of Prussia mall (10 min).
culture.socialScene: Small selective campus (7,000). Main Line culture is affluent and polished. Strong Catholic identity. Philadelphia access via commuter rail means the city is always available.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Villanova is prestigious and Philadelphia Medical is elite for PT pathway. Soccer is in transition — lowest Big East program tier. Very expensive. Best if academic prestige, CHOP/Penn Medicine clinical access, and Philadelphia lifestyle are priorities.
culture.lifestyleTags: Main Line prestige, Philadelphia access, CHOP clinical

staff.name: Mark Fetrow
staff.bg: Head Coach. Developing Villanova program. Philadelphia medical district access is the key academic draw.

rec: Prestigious small Catholic university. Philadelphia suburb with strong Main Line culture. Expensive for the program tier.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 3 consecutive last-place East Division finishes mean competition quality is lower. 4 MFs clear. 3 rising juniors provide 2 years of competition before departing. Philadelphia medical district (CHOP, Penn Medicine) is the academic draw despite weak soccer.

name: Marquette
acuAlignNote: Exercise Science covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics. Froedtert & Medical College of Wisconsin provides pre-PT clinical access.

fin.internationalNote: Marquette full COA is ~$74k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–50% of COA. Catholic mission scholarship may stack. Milwaukee cost of living is moderate. Merit aid available for international students with strong academic records.

gpa.note: Jesuit university. Athletic scholarship pathway established.

confRecord.note: Lost to Villanova 0-1 on final day.
Big East conference play. NCAA Tournament history.

coach.name: David Korn
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Ami Bhatt has built Marquette into a consistent Big East mid-table competitor with multiple conference titles. His program consistently punches above its size. Froedtert & Medical College of Wisconsin partnership provides pre-PT clinical access unique in the Big East. Milwaukee city environment with strong Jesuit academic culture.

facilityDetails.stadium: Valley Fields — 1,750 capacity, natural grass stadium + adjacent FieldTurf Revolution 360 practice field. On-campus in Milwaukee.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Valley Fields training pitch complex. Indoor access at Al McGuire Center in winter.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Marquette Athletics S&C facility — full suite. Renovated 2022.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Exercise Science Department labs. Froedtert Health + Medical College of Wisconsin research partnership.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Froedtert Health (official Marquette Athletics partner) — team physicians on-site. Medical College of Wisconsin (MCW) — ranked T-50 USA — provides pre-PT/Chiro clinical access.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Exercise Science labs: exercise physiology, biomechanics, motor learning. MCW and Froedtert Health clinical placements available. Strong pre-PT pathway — Marquette has DPT articulation history.
facilityDetails.extras: Milwaukee — genuine mid-size city with strong professional and cultural scene. Fiserv Forum (NBA Bucks), American Family Field (MLB Brewers), Milwaukee Art Museum, Lake Michigan waterfront. Chicago (90 min drive) adds major-city proximity.
facilityDetails.note: KBS Soccer Pavilion (2011, 3,000sqft) — dedicated locker rooms for men's and women's programs. $3.6M seasonal dome (2017) over FieldTurf field for climate-controlled winter training and competition. Strong year-round training setup.

culture.vibe: Jesuit Catholic university — academic rigour, community service ethos, Milwaukee city campus. Marquette basketball (Final Four history) dominates the athletic culture but soccer punches above its weight. Strong alumni network (law, business, health professions).
culture.thingsToDo: Milwaukee Bucks (NBA, Fiserv Forum), Milwaukee Brewers (MLB), Summerfest (world's largest music festival), Lake Michigan waterfront, Milwaukee Art Museum, Third Ward arts district, Chicago day trip (90 min). Moderate cost of living.
culture.socialScene: Medium Jesuit campus (8,000). Downtown Milwaukee — walkable to restaurants, venues. Strong Catholic and community-service culture. Good international student community.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Marquette is the most balanced Big East option below the elite tier. Solid soccer (real minutes), Froedtert/MCW clinical access, Jesuit degree value, and moderate cost all align well. Cold winters are the main trade-off vs Olivier's warm-climate preference.
culture.lifestyleTags: Milwaukee city, Froedtert clinical, Jesuit culture

staff.name: Ami Bhatt
staff.bg: Head Coach. Multiple Big East titles. Program consistently mid-table in Big East.

rec: Solid Big East program in Milwaukee. Consistent tournament contender. Cold winters but real city life and Froedtert Health clinical access.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 of 8 MFs cleared — no rising seniors in Yr1, only 3 juniors from 2025-26 Fr. class. Marquette will recruit actively (Medium-High risk). Program note: Tristan Ronnestad-Stevens (Perth, AUS) on 2025-26 squad — Australian presence established. Head coach confirmed: David Korn. Froedtert/MCW clinical access is the pre-PT draw.

name: Georgetown

confRecord.note: Big East Regular Season + Tournament Champions. NCAA Quarterfinals. East Regional Staff of Year.
Big East Tournament Champions 2024. Consistent top-2 Big East.
Strong Big East season. NCAA appearance.
NCAA Tournament. Consistent Wiese era dominance.
Big East champions. NCAA College Cup semifinal.
Big East season champions — COVID-altered season.

titles: Big East Regular Season Champions — 8 times under Wiese

proPlayers.draftRank: Strong — 42 MLS signings under Wiese all-time
notable: 42 MLS signings total under Brian Wiese
Multiple NYCFC and DC United connections

acRank: Strong — B.S. Exercise Science with explicit PT/OT preparation
kinRank: B.S. Exercise Science — Kinesiology & Health Studies dept with pre-PT advising track
acuAlignNote: Strong alignment — Georgetown's Exercise Science degree covers anatomy, exercise physiology, biomechanics, sport psychology, motor control, research methods and nutrition. Pre-PT advising track explicitly prepares students for DPT admission. DC location provides world-class clinical placement opportunities (Georgetown Hospital, MedStar, GW Hospital). Misses some ACU-specific advanced units and professional experience hours format differs.
soccerLevel: Big East — D1 Elite. 2019 National Champions. Perennial top-10 program.
aid: Athletic + Need-based (Georgetown meets 100% of demonstrated need)
prePT: Excellent — DC medical network + Georgetown Hospital clinical access

gpa.minSchol: Georgetown meets 100% of need — GPA drives academic standing
gpa.note: Georgetown is one of the most selective universities in the US (17% acceptance rate). GPA 2.8 is a significant challenge. An athletic scholarship and improving GPA trajectory are essential. Georgetown does offer need-based aid that can substantially reduce cost — worth exploring even with high sticker price.

fin.internationalNote: Georgetown meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for all admitted students including internationals. Official 2025-26 undergraduate COA is $97,264/yr (continuing students). Athletic scholarship + need-based aid can substantially reduce COA. Requires financial disclosure during application.

culture.vibe: Elite private Jesuit university in Washington D.C. — one of the most prestigious universities in the US. Soccer is a genuine passion on campus — Wiese has built a powerhouse with a devoted fanbase. Georgetown's location in D.C. is unmatched for internships, networking, and clinical placements.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ D.C. is a world-class city with international culture, excellent food, public transit, and a strong Australian expat community. Not a warm climate (cold winters). The soccer program is elite and Wiese's style suits a technically gifted midfielder. Academically demanding — GPA 2.8 is the main barrier.
culture.socialScene: Cosmopolitan D.C. campus. Georgetown neighborhood is historic and vibrant — bars, restaurants, waterfront on the Potomac. Strong international student community. Political, media, and legal culture dominant on campus.
culture.thingsToDo: Washington D.C. — Smithsonian museums (free), National Mall, Capitol Hill, Georgetown waterfront, Rock Creek Park, Nationals baseball, DC United (MLS), Capitals hockey. NYC 4hr, Philadelphia 2.5hr. Beach access via Chesapeake Bay.
culture.lifestyleTags: World-class city, elite university, cold winters, international culture

facilityDetails.stadium: Shaw Field — 1,625 capacity. On-campus, natural grass. D.C. atmosphere unique.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Multiple training fields on and near campus. Cooper Field primary training. D.C. winter limits outdoor training Oct-Feb.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Georgetown Athletics S&C — well resourced for a small private university. Full weight room, Olympic platforms, GPS integration (Catapult).
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Video analysis (Hudl), GPS tracking, heart rate monitoring. Georgetown invests heavily in player development analytics.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Georgetown's health sciences facilities are world-class — direct access to Georgetown University Hospital (one of DC's top academic medical centres) for clinical placement. Exercise Science labs include biomechanics, exercise physiology, and motor control equipment.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Georgetown Athletics full-time AT staff. Georgetown University Hospital proximity means access to some of the best sports medicine and physical therapy clinicians in the US for clinical observation hours.
facilityDetails.extras: D.C. internship access (political, media, NGO, healthcare) is unmatched. Georgetown neighborhood — one of the most desirable in America. Proximity to major MLS market (DC United).
facilityDetails.note: Georgetown's location in D.C. is its unique differentiator — access to world-class hospitals for clinical hours, MLS connections through DC United, and an elite academic environment. The trade-off is cost and cold winters.

coach.name: Brian Wiese
coach.title: Head Men's Soccer Coach
coach.profile: Brian Wiese — 20th season at Georgetown. 2019 NCAA National Champion. 42 MLS signings all-time. 6× East Regional Staff of Year (2025, 2024, 2021, 2019, 2018, 2010). 14 NCAA Tournament berths in 16-year span. Known for technical, possession-based style. Former assistant at Stanford and Notre Dame. Staff: Connor Klekota (Asst HC), Michael Montross (Asst), Brian Bouhl (Asst/Dir Ops).

rec: Georgetown is an elite D.C. program — 2019 national champions, perennial top-10, world-class city location with unmatched clinical placement access. The Exercise Science degree explicitly prepares for DPT programs. The challenge is GPA (2.8 is borderline for admission to a 17% acceptance rate university) and cost ($85k sticker though need-based aid can help). If Olivier's GPA improves to 3.0+ and he can secure an athletic scholarship, Georgetown offers one of the best combinations of soccer, academics, and PT pathway in the country.

minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Georgetown is a perennial Big East champion with a deep, technically elite squad. MF competition is intense. Wiese recruits nationally and internationally — Olivier would need to be exceptional to break in early. Years 3-4 are the realistic peak opportunity.
